papers.title: Molecular characterization of three GIBBERELLIN-INSENSITIVE DWARF1 homologous genes in hexaploid wheat
papers.authors: Li A, Yang W, Li S, Liu D, Guo X, Sun J, Zhang A

papers.title: Dominant and Pleiotropic Effects of a GAI Gene in Wheat Results from a Lack of Interaction between DELLA and GID1
papers.authors: Wu J, Kong X, Wan J, Liu X, Zhang X, Guo X, Zhou R, Zhao G, Jing R, Fu X, Jia J

edges.mechanism: GA3 binds TaGID1 homeologs; binding strengthens DELLA interaction (Y2H)
edges.evidence_sentence: Strong interactions between each putative TaGID1 and the wheat DELLA proteins RHT-A1, RHT-B1, and RHT-D1 in the presence of GA3, and weak interactions in the absence of GA3

edges.mechanism: Negative feedback on TaGID1 expression: exogenous GA3 down-regulates TaGID1 transcripts
edges.evidence_sentence: down-regulated after treatment with exogenous gibberellic acid (GA3), indicating feedback control

edges.mechanism: GA-bound TaGID1 targets DELLA for proteasomal degradation; Rht-B1c insertion blocks GID1 binding preventing degradation
edges.evidence_sentence: 30-amino acid insertion in the DELLAALGYKV motif physically blocks the GID1 recognition sequence

edges.mechanism: TaGID1 promotes DELLA degradation, relieving growth repression; OE rescues gid1 dwarf in Arabidopsis
edges.evidence_sentence: Over-expression of each TaGID1 gene in the Arabidopsis double mutant gid1a/1c partially rescued the dwarf phenotype

perturbations.evidence_sentence: Over-expression of each TaGID1 gene in the Arabidopsis double mutant gid1a/1c partially rescued the dwarf phenotype
perturbations.notes: Heterologous rescue demonstrating GA receptor function

perturbations.evidence_sentence: Transgenic rice expressing Rht-B1c showed extreme dwarf stature with 80.9% reduction in height versus 23.8% for the semidominant Rht-B1b allele
perturbations.notes: Dominant Rht-B1c blocks GID1 binding; extreme dwarf in rice

perturbations.evidence_sentence: Wild-type seedlings increased 30% with GA3 treatment
perturbations.notes: GA3 treatment of WT wheat promotes elongation

perturbations.evidence_sentence: Rht-B1c showed only 5% non-significant increase, confirming complete GA insensitivity
perturbations.notes: GA3 cannot rescue Rht-B1c dwarf (no GID1 interaction)
